perturbation.original_text: Any dispute arising in connection with this Agreement, including any dispute related to the validity, interpretation, implementation and/or termination of the Agreement, which cannot be amicably settled within the sixty (60) days following its occurrence shall be finally settled under the Rules of Arbitration of the International Chamber of Commerce by one arbitrator appointed in accordance with those Rules. The arbitration proceedings shall take place in Paris (France). The language to be used in the arbitral proceedings shall be English. NDA – EUNCL & Sponsor 5/6 The applicable law shall be Belgian Law, under which the dispute, controversy or claim referred to arbitration shall be decided without regard to its conflict of laws principles.
perturbation.changed_text: Any notices for technical correspondence in connection with the Agreement shall be sent to: Mrs Fanny CAPUTO ……………, if to CEA CEA Grenoble/LETI/DTBS 17 avenue des Martyrs – 38054 Grenoble Cedex 09 Tel: 04 38 78 15 06 Email: fanny.caputo@cea.fr _________________________ if to __________ ________________________________________ ________________________________________ Tel: ___________________ Email: ___________________ Any dispute arising in connection with this Agreement, including any dispute related to the validity, interpretation, implementation and/or termination of the Agreement, which cannot be amicably settled within the sixty (60) days following its occurrence shall be finally settled under the Rules of Arbitration of the International Chamber of Commerce by one arbitrator appointed in accordance with those Rules. The arbitration proceedings shall take place in Paris (France). The language to be used in the arbitral proceedings shall be English. NDA – EUNCL & Sponsor 5/6 The applicable law shall be Belgian Law, under which the dispute, controversy or claim referred to arbitration shall be decided without regard to its conflict of laws principles.
perturbation.explanation: By placing the dispute resolution clause within the section for 'notices for technical correspondence,' it becomes less conspicuous and harder for a party to readily identify their rights and obligations regarding dispute resolution. This obfuscation impacts the enforceability of the arbitration clause.
perturbation.law_citation: Article 1676 of the Belgian Judicial Code
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 1676 of the Belgian Judicial Code addresses arbitration agreements. While it doesn't explicitly mandate a specific section for arbitration clauses, placing such a critical clause in an unrelated section like 'notices' could be interpreted as lacking the necessary prominence and clarity, especially if it's argued that this placement was intended to obscure the clause. This undermines the principle that arbitration agreements should be clear and understood by all parties, potentially leading to challenges regarding the validity and enforceability of the clause under Belgian law.

perturbation.original_text: The Confidential Information is disclosed, “as is”. The Disclosing Party makes no representations or warranties, whether express or implied, with respect to Confidential Information, and notably their fitness for a commercial technical purpose. The use of Confidential Information is made at the sole risk of the Receiving Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 11 - The Agreement cancels and supersedes all previous written or oral agreements and understandings between the Parties with regard to the Purpose, and constitutes the Parties’ entire agreement as to such Purpose. No addition or modification of the terms of the Agreement shall be valid between the Parties unless made in writing and signed by their duly authorised representatives. The Confidential Information is disclosed, “as is”. The Disclosing Party makes no representations or warranties, whether express or implied, with respect to Confidential Information, and notably their fitness for a commercial technical purpose. The use of Confidential Information is made at the sole risk of the Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the 'as is' disclaimer to the 'entire agreement' section makes it less conspicuous and separates it from the clauses detailing the responsibilities around confidentiality. This could weaken its effect, making it more difficult for the disclosing party to argue that the receiving party accepted the information without any warranties.
perturbation.law_citation: Article 1134 of the Belgian Civil Code
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 1134 of the Belgian Civil Code emphasizes the binding nature of agreements lawfully entered into. By burying a critical disclaimer like the 'as is' warranty within the boilerplate 'entire agreement' clause, one could argue it's a hidden or less obvious term. If it is considered that one party didn't understand they waived the warranty in accepting data, it could be seen as contradicting the spirit of good faith and fair dealing required for legally binding agreements under Article 1134.

perturbation.original_text: Disclosure period: this Agreement governs communication of Confidential Information from ________________________ (the “Effective Date”) for twelve (12) month duration and shall then terminate. The Receiving Party’s duty to protect Confidential Information as foreseen in article 4 above shall continue for a period of five (5) years after expiry or termination.
perturbation.changed_text: The Agreement cancels and supersedes all previous written or oral agreements and understandings between the Parties with regard to the Purpose, and constitutes the Parties’ entire agreement as to such Purpose. No addition or modification of the terms of the Agreement shall be valid between the Parties unless made in writing and signed by their duly authorised representatives. Disclosure period: this Agreement governs communication of Confidential Information from ________________________ (the “Effective Date”) for twelve (12) month duration and shall then terminate. The Receiving Party’s duty to protect Confidential Information as foreseen in article 4 above shall continue for a period of five (5) years after expiry or termination.
perturbation.explanation: Relocating the 'disclosure period' clause to be within the 'entire agreement' section obscures the explicit timeframe for confidential information exchange and protection. This could create ambiguity and weaken the enforceability of the confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Belgian Law on Trade Secrets, Article XI.331
perturbation.law_citation: Article XI.331 of the Belgian Code of Economic Law
perturbation.law_explanation: Article XI.331 outlines measures to protect trade secrets. While it doesn't explicitly mandate the location of the duration clause, placing it within the 'entire agreement' clause, rather than in a section that clearly defines the scope and period of confidentiality, could weaken the argument that reasonable steps were taken to protect the trade secret, especially if the duration of protection is contested.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Justel databank Andere informatie en diensten van de overheid: www.belgium.be Naar boven Naar boven Titel 2013201449 28 FEVRIER 2013. - Arrêté du Gouvernement wallon modifiant l'arrêté du Gouvernement wallon du 9 septembre 2004 relatif à l'application du prélèvement dans le secteur du lait et des produits laitiers Bron: SERVICE PUBLIC DE WALLONIE Publicatie: 12 maart 2013 Nummer: 2013201449 bladzijde: 14668 Dossiernummer: 2013-02-28/03 Inwerkingtreding : 13 maart 2013 Deze tekst wijzigt de volgende tekst: 2004203314 Inhoudstafel Art. 1-3 Tekst Article 1er . A l'article 15, § 1er, 7°, de l'arrêté du Gouvernement wallon du 9 septembre 2004 relatif à l'application du prélèvement dans le secteur du lait et des produits laitiers, complété par l'arrêté du Gouvernement wallon du 19 décembre 2008, le c) est remplacé par ce qui suit : " c) Lorsque la somme des quantités à réallouer aux producteurs visés en a et b est inférieure à la somme des quantités qui ont fait l'objet d'une demande de libération, chaque demande de libération est réduite en fonction de l'importance relative des litres en surplus non réalloués, comme suit. Par producteur, la quantité non libérée est obtenue en multipliant la demande initiale par le ratio somme des litres non réalloués divisée par la somme des litres qui ont fait l'objet d'une demande de libération. Les litres non libérés ainsi obtenus sont restitués à leur titulaire. " Art. 2 . Le présent arrêté entre en vigueur le lendemain du jour de sa publication au Moniteur belge. Art. 3. Le Ministre de l'Agriculture est chargé de l'exécution du présent arrêté. Namur, le 28 février 2013. Le Ministre-Président, R. DEMOTTE Le Ministre des Travaux publics, de l'Agriculture, de la Ruralité, de la Nature, de la Forêt et du Patrimoine, C. DI ANTONIO Aanhef Le Gouvernement wallon, Vu la loi du 28 mars 1975 relative au commerce des produits de l'agriculture, de l'horticulture et de la pêche maritime, l'article 3, § 1er, 1°, modifié par la loi du 29 décembre 1990; Vu l'arrêté du Gouvernement wallon du 9 septembre 2004 relatif à l'application du prélèvement dans le secteur du lait et des produits laitiers; Vu l'urgence motivée par la nécessité de faire entrer en vigueur la modification envisagée dans les meilleurs délais et ce, au plus tard avant la clôture de la campagne laitière 2012-2013 qui intervient au 31 mars 2013; Vu l'avis 52.849/4 du Conseil d'Etat, donné le 13 février 2013, en application de l'article 84, § 1er, alinéa 1er, 2°, des lois sur le Conseil d'Etat, coordonnées le 12 janvier 1973; Considérant que l'article 15, § 1er, 7°, c), de l'arrêté du Gouvernement wallon du 9 septembre 2004 a été modifié par l'article 7 de l'arrêté du Gouvernement wallon du 19 décembre 2008; Considérant que l'objet de cette modification était de préciser qu'en cas d'excédent de libérations par rapport aux demandes de réallocation, l'excédent des quotas non réalloués devait être restitué à leur titulaire; Considérant que le texte de cette disposition prévoit en réalité que la quantité à libérer est obtenue en multipliant la demande initiale par le ratio somme des litres non réalloués divisée par la somme des litres qui ont fait l'objet d'une demande de libération; Considérant dès lors que cette disposition n'a pas été correctement formulée, de sorte que les producteurs libérateurs de quotas devraient se voir restituer plus de litres que nécessaire, et ce, au détriment des bénéficiaires de la réallocation qui devraient recevoir, en conséquence, moins de litres; Considérant qu'il convient de procéder à la modification de cette disposition pour que la formule de calcul prévue puisse être correctement exécutée; Sur la proposition du Ministre de l'Agriculture; Après délibération, Arrête : Links https://www.ejustice.just.fgov.be/eli/arrete/2013/02/28/2013201449/justel Link kopiëren Beeld van de officiële publicatie Externe link Raad van State

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party undertakes during the term of this Agreement and for a period of five (5) years after the date of its expiry or termination: NDA – EUNCL & Sponsor 4/6 - to safeguard Confidential Information as it does for its own proprietary information of like importance, but at least with a reasonable degree of care, - to use Confidential Information only for the Purpose and to divulge Confidential Information to its personnel for said Purpose only and on a "need to know" basis, - not to duplicate or otherwise reproduce Confidential Information except for such copies as the Receiving Party may require for the Purpose as aforesaid, provided that all copies shall contain the same proprietary and confidential notices and legends as appear on the original Confidential Information, - not to divulge Confidential Information to any third party for any purpose, unless and until expressly authorized in writing to do so by the Disclosing Party, - not to reverse-compile, reverse-assemble or reverse-engineer Confidential Information or any part thereof, unless directed to do so by the disclosing Party as proven by appropriate documentation - not to claim nor to register any Intellectual Property right, nor to exercise any Intellectual Property right or any other right on Confidential Information received under the Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Any notices for technical correspondence in connection with the Agreement shall be sent to: Mrs Fanny CAPUTO ……………, if to CEA CEA Grenoble/LETI/DTBS 17 avenue des Martyrs – 38054 Grenoble Cedex 09 Tel: 04 38 78 15 06 Email: fanny.caputo@cea.fr _________________________ if to __________ ________________________________________ ________________________________________ Tel: ___________________ Email: ___________________ The Receiving Party undertakes during the term of this Agreement and for a period of five (5) years after the date of its expiry or termination: NDA – EUNCL & Sponsor 4/6 - to safeguard Confidential Information as it does for its own proprietary information of like importance, but at least with a reasonable degree of care, - to use Confidential Information only for the Purpose and to divulge Confidential Information to its personnel for said Purpose only and on a "need to know" basis, - not to duplicate or otherwise reproduce Confidential Information except for such copies as the Receiving Party may require for the Purpose as aforesaid, provided that all copies shall contain the same proprietary and confidential notices and legends as appear on the original Confidential Information, - not to divulge Confidential Information to any third party for any purpose, unless and until expressly authorized in writing to do so by the Disclosing Party, - not to reverse-compile, reverse-assemble or reverse-engineer Confidential Information or any part thereof, unless directed to do so by the disclosing Party as proven by appropriate documentation - not to claim nor to register any Intellectual Property right, nor to exercise any Intellectual Property right or any other right on Confidential Information received under the Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the core confidentiality obligations to the 'notices for technical correspondence' section buries the Receiving Party's specific duties within boilerplate contact information. This makes it harder for the Disclosing Party to demonstrate that the Receiving Party was adequately informed about their obligations, thereby weakening the enforceability of the confidentiality provisions.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Belgian Law on Trade Secrets, Article XI.331
perturbation.law_citation: Article XI.331 of the Belgian Code of Economic Law
perturbation.law_explanation: Article XI.331 requires the trade secret holder to take reasonable steps to keep information secret. By burying the Receiving Party's key confidentiality obligations in an unrelated section such as 'Notices', the Disclosing Party may have difficulty proving they took sufficient measures to ensure the Receiving Party was aware of and bound by these obligations. If the obligations are considered too obscure to reasonably be seen and understood, the argument to claim trade secret protection may weaken.

perturbation.original_text: The Agreement is personal to the Parties (« intuitu personae ») and the Receiving Party undertakes not to assign nor transfer its rights or obligations under the Agreement to any third party, including an affiliated company, without the Disclosing Party’s prior written approval.
perturbation.changed_text: Any notices for technical correspondence in connection with the Agreement shall be sent to: Mrs Fanny CAPUTO ……………, if to CEA CEA Grenoble/LETI/DTBS 17 avenue des Martyrs – 38054 Grenoble Cedex 09 Tel: 04 38 78 15 06 Email: fanny.caputo@cea.fr _________________________ if to __________ ________________________________________ ________________________________________ Tel: ___________________ Email: ___________________ The Agreement is personal to the Parties (« intuitu personae ») and the Receiving Party undertakes not to assign nor transfer its rights or obligations under the Agreement to any third party, including an affiliated company, without the Disclosing Party’s prior written approval.
perturbation.explanation: By moving the clause restricting assignment to the section on 'notices for technical correspondence,' it becomes less prominent. This could be argued as making it harder for the Disclosing Party to enforce the non-assignment provision.
perturbation.law_citation: Article 1690 of the Belgian Civil Code
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 1690 of the Belgian Civil Code governs the assignment of debts and other incorporeal rights. While the article focuses on the transfer of rights to third parties with notification being key, burying a crucial clause that *prevents* assignment within the 'Notices' section diminishes its prominence and clarity. A Receiving Party could claim that the limitation on assignment was not adequately brought to their attention, potentially challenging the enforceability of the restriction, and claiming the assigning party has no way to know this since this information is buried.
